sweeps.summary: Quiet morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, and xAI/OpenAI/Anthropic/Google lab blogs all checked. xAI's Grok STT/TTS combined announcement (April 17) and Google's A2UI v0.9 (April 17) were identified but fall outside the 6-hour window. No new releases from the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar.

Quiet late-evening window on a Saturday — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, and xAI/OpenAI/Anthropic/Google blogs all checked. Grok 4.3 beta (xAI, April 17) was identified but falls outside the 6-hour window and is locked to $300/month SuperGrok Heavy. No new releases from the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar.

Quiet evening window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, and lab blogs all checked. EvoMap/evolver (4.9k stars, v1.67.6 released today) is trending but its notable event — an MIT-to-GPLv3 license change after a plagiarism dispute with NousResearch — happened April 15 and has no announcement post to link today. No new releases from the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar.

Quiet midday window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers all checked. GPT-Rosalind (April 16, life-sciences reasoning model) and Perplexity Personal Computer (April 16, Mac desktop agent) were identified but fall outside the 6-hour window and were previously evaluated. Cerebras IPO S-1 filing (April 17) is financial news without a product/code impact. No new releases from the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar.

Quiet late-morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, Anthropic/Mistral/xAI/DeepMind blogs all checked. GitHub trending was led by EvoMap/evolver (v1.67.4 bug fix, not notable), BasedHardware/omi (minor v0.11.333 patch), and google/magika (2024-era project resurgent in stars). No new releases from the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar.

Quiet mid-morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, Cloudflare Agents Week updates page, and lab blogs all checked. GitHub trending was led by obra/superpowers (last release March 31), lsdefine/GenericAgent (January origin), and openai/openai-agents-python (already in feed). GPT-Rosalind (OpenAI life sciences model, April 16) was identified but falls outside the 6-hour window and was already evaluated by the 10:49Z sweep yesterday. No new releases from the past 6 hours cleared the bar.

Quiet early-morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, and Anthropic/OpenAI/Mistral blogs all checked. GitHub trending was led by EvoMap/evolver (4.5k stars, v1.67.4 released today but a February-origin project with no announcement blog) and BasedHardware/omi (v0.11.331 minor patch). No new releases from the last 6 hours passed the notability bar.

Two additions this sweep: Mozilla Thunderbolt (MZLA's open-source self-hostable enterprise AI client, 1.1k GitHub stars) and Mistral Connectors going API/SDK-accessible with MCP support and human-in-the-loop approvals.

added.title: Mozilla Thunderbolt — Open-Source Self-Hostable Enterprise AI Client
added.note: MZLA/Mozilla released an open-source cross-platform enterprise AI client with MCP, Ollama, and Haystack support — a genuinely notable self-hostable alternative to proprietary enterprise AI stacks, with 1.1k stars and coverage in The Register and OMG Ubuntu.

added.title: Mistral Connectors — MCP and Built-in Data Sources via API and SDK
added.note: Mistral opened its Studio connectors (GitHub, Gmail, web search, custom MCP servers) to API/SDK access with direct tool calling and human-in-the-loop approvals — a meaningful upgrade for teams building agentic workflows programmatically.

sweeps.summary: Quiet overnight window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, Anthropic/OpenAI/Mistral/DeepMind blogs all checked. GitHub trending was led by obra/superpowers (last release March 31), lsdefine/GenericAgent (January 16), and openai/openai-agents-python (already in feed). HF trending models were all already in the feed. No new releases from the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar.

Quiet late-evening window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers, and lab blogs (Anthropic, Mistral, Google/DeepMind) all checked. The leading HN AI post was Claude Design (already in feed); top GitHub trending repos were either existing feed items or below the notability bar (EvoMap/evolver at 4.1K stars, BasedHardware/omi with a minor v0.11.330 patch). No new releases passed the quality bar.

One addition from tonight's window — NVIDIA Nemotron OCR v2, announced in an official HuggingFace blog post today: a unified open-weights OCR model that handles six languages at 34.7 pages/second on a single A100, 28x faster than PaddleOCR v5, with the full 12.26M-image synthetic training dataset released under CC-BY-4.0.

added.title: NVIDIA Nemotron OCR v2 — Unified Multilingual OCR, 28x Faster Than PaddleOCR
added.note: Official NVIDIA HuggingFace blog post published April 17; fast unified open-weights OCR covering six languages with a single 84M-parameter model — notable for ML practitioners working with multilingual documents at scale.

sweeps.summary: One addition: Canva AI 2.0, launched April 16 at Canva Create — 220M-user design platform's first agentic product, turning a text brief into fully layered editable designs with brand memory and workflow integrations for Slack, Gmail, and Zoom. All other candidates (GitHub trending, HN, HuggingFace) were already in the feed.

added.title: Canva AI 2.0 — Agentic Design Platform with Prompt-to-Design, Memory, and Workflow Integrations
added.note: Launched April 16 at Canva Create in Los Angeles — Canva's biggest product change since 2013, offering prompt-based design generation, persistent brand memory, and enterprise workflow integrations in research preview to the first 1M users.

sweeps.summary: Quiet late-afternoon window — GitHub trending, HN front page, and HuggingFace papers and trending models all checked. All notable April 17 items (Claude Design) and April 16 items (Claude Opus 4.7, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, Cloudflare Agents Week, DeepL Voice-to-Voice, Android CLI, HY-World 2.0) were already in the feed from prior sweeps.

Added Claude Design by Anthropic Labs via manual proposal workflow. Anthropic's new visual creation product — prototypes, wireframes, pitch decks powered by Opus 4.7 — launched in research preview today.

added.title: Claude Design by Anthropic Labs — AI-Powered Visual Creation in Research Preview
added.note: Official Anthropic Labs launch of a new visual design product powered by Opus 4.7, announced April 17 2026 with notable early adopters including Canva, Brilliant, and Datadog.

sweeps.summary: Quiet midday window — GitHub trending, HN front page, and HuggingFace papers and models all checked. The highest-signal HN story ('Codex for almost everything', 918 points) resolves to the same April 16 Codex update already in the feed; all other trending repos and papers were either already covered or below the bar.

Quiet late-morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, and HuggingFace papers all checked. OpenAI's GPT-Rosalind (life sciences model, April 16) was identified as a candidate but had to be dropped: the official openai.com URL returns 403 to bots and no verified image URL could be found after multiple fetch attempts.

Quiet mid-morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, HuggingFace trending models and papers all checked. No new releases from the past 6 hours cleared the bar; all notable April 17 items (Claude Opus 4.7, Codex, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B) were already in the feed from prior sweeps.

Quiet early-morning window — GitHub trending, HN front page, and HuggingFace all checked. All notable April 16 releases (Claude Opus 4.7, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, Codex, Cloudflare Agents Week) were already in the feed from prior sweeps.

Two Agents Week additions from April 16 not caught by prior sweeps: Cloudflare AI Search (formerly AutoRAG, now a managed hybrid RAG service with MCP endpoint) and DeepL Voice-to-Voice, the first voice product from the translation API that 200,000+ businesses use.

added.title: Cloudflare AI Search — Managed Hybrid Search Primitive for AI Agents
added.note: Released April 16 as part of Cloudflare Agents Week; the relaunch of AutoRAG as a fully managed hybrid search service with built-in MCP endpoint fills a real gap for agent developers who don't want to manage a separate vector database.

added.title: DeepL Voice-to-Voice — Real-Time Speech Translation Suite
added.note: Launched April 16 — DeepL's first voice product from a translation company with 200,000+ business customers, developer API included, and 96% linguist preference over Google/Microsoft/Zoom in blind evaluation.

sweeps.summary: Two solid drops from the overnight window: Google's Android CLI suite for agent-driven Android development (3× faster, 70% fewer tokens), and Tencent's HY-World 2.0 — the first open-source 3D world model from a major lab, converting text and video into game-engine-ready 3D assets.

added.title: Android CLI — Build Android Apps 3× Faster with AI Agents
added.note: Official Google launch on April 16 with verified concrete metrics — 3× faster and 70% fewer tokens for agent-driven Android development, backed by an official blog post and docs page.

added.title: HY-World 2.0 — Tencent's Open-Source Multi-Modal 3D World Generation and Reconstruction
added.note: Released April 16 on GitHub with 781 stars — first open-source 3D world model from a major lab that outputs game-engine-importable geometry (Blender, Unity, Unreal, Isaac Sim) instead of video.

sweeps.summary: Two items from the April 16 evening window: NousResearch Hermes Agent v0.10.0 (93K-star self-growing agent framework with new Tool Gateway) and Physical Intelligence π0.7 (generalist robot model showing early compositional generalization).

added.title: Hermes Agent v0.10.0 — Tool Gateway for Self-Growing Multi-Platform AI Agents
added.note: Trending at 93K+ stars on GitHub today with v0.10.0 launching a subscription-based Tool Gateway — notable self-growing agent framework from Nous Research.

added.title: π0.7 — Physical Intelligence's Generalist Robot Brain with Compositional Generalization
added.note: Published April 16 by Physical Intelligence, π0.7 shows early compositional generalization — first published result that their generalist model matches task-specific specialists without fine-tuning.

sweeps.summary: Added Claude Opus 4.7 via manual proposal workflow. Anthropic's new flagship model released today with 3x agentic coding throughput and significantly improved vision resolution.

added.title: Claude Opus 4.7 — Anthropic's New Flagship with Step-Change Agentic Coding
added.note: Released April 16, 2026 — Anthropic's new flagship model with 3x agentic coding throughput, 3.75-megapixel vision, and 1M token context at unchanged $5/$25 pricing.

sweeps.summary: One item from last night's window missed by all prior sweeps: Codex CLI 0.121.0, which adds git-hosted plugin marketplace installs, Ctrl+R TUI prompt history, per-mode memory controls, and MCP parallel-call support across 70+ PRs.

added.title: OpenAI Codex CLI v0.121.0 — Marketplace Plugins, TUI History, and Memory Controls
added.note: Released April 15 at 20:45 UTC but missed by all prior sweeps; the new marketplace command changes Codex extensibility by letting teams publish plugins as plain git repos without npm.

sweeps.summary: One solid drop from academia: SpatialEvo from ZJU + StepFun lands on HuggingFace with 3B and 7B Apache 2.0 weights, a novel self-evolving approach to 3D spatial reasoning that skips model-consensus pseudo-labels entirely and uses deterministic geometry as its reward oracle.

added.title: SpatialEvo — Self-Evolving 3D Spatial Reasoning with Deterministic Geometric Environments
added.note: Released today on arXiv + HuggingFace with open weights (3B and 7B, Apache 2.0) and code; addresses a real training failure mode in spatial reasoning by grounding rewards in computable geometry rather than model consensus.

sweeps.summary: Two solid drops: Google launches its first-ever native Gemini desktop app for Mac (global, free, macOS 15+), and IBM Research publishes VAKRA, an executable enterprise agent benchmark with 8,000+ live APIs showing current frontier models still have a long way to go.

added.title: Gemini for Mac — Google's Native macOS Desktop App Launches Globally
added.note: Google's first Gemini desktop app ships today globally — native macOS integration with system-wide Option+Space shortcut and live screen sharing, a real step beyond browser-tab AI.

added.title: VAKRA — IBM Research's Enterprise Agent Benchmark with 8,000+ Live APIs
added.note: Published today by IBM Research on HuggingFace: a replay-based, executable enterprise agent benchmark with live APIs and a public leaderboard — rare in a space dominated by static eval sets.

sweeps.summary: One solid drop from OpenAI: Agents SDK v0.14.0 ships Sandbox Agents in beta, giving long-horizon agents a persistent isolated workspace with filesystem, shell, and cloud storage — a meaningful infrastructure upgrade for Python developers building production agents.

added.title: OpenAI Agents SDK v0.14.0 — Sandbox Agents with Persistent Isolated Workspaces
added.note: Released today at 17:11 UTC by OpenAI — a substantial new beta capability for the 20.8k-star Agents SDK that standardizes long-horizon agent infrastructure with swappable backends, covered by TechCrunch and The New Stack.

sweeps.summary: Quiet evening window — checked GitHub trending, HackerNews, HuggingFace papers, and major lab blogs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, xAI). Adobe announced Firefly AI Assistant today but it's a 'coming soon' beta, not a shipped product. No other new releases cleared the bar.
Light sweep — one hot drop from Google: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS Preview lands with inline audio tags, letting developers embed [whispers] or [excited] directly in text to control delivery without SSML.

added.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS Preview — Google's Inline Audio-Tag Text-to-Speech
added.note: New Google TTS model released today with a genuinely novel feature: inline audio expression tags embedded directly in text, replacing SSML for controlling tone and delivery.

sweeps.summary: Expanded sweep covering security AI, medical AI, enterprise AI, and trending repos. Highlights: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit (runtime security for AI agents), Amazon Bio Discovery (drug discovery), Epic AI Agents (healthcare automation), NVIDIA Agent Toolkit (enterprise AI platform), plus Voicebox (open-source voice synthesis) and Kronos (financial foundation model).

added.title: Amazon Bio Discovery — AI-Powered Drug Discovery on AWS
added.note: AWS enters pharma AI with a no-code platform for drug molecule generation using biological foundation models.

added.title: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit — Runtime Security for AI Agents
added.note: First toolkit addressing all 10 OWASP Agentic AI risks with sub-millisecond policy enforcement. MIT licensed.

added.title: NVIDIA Agent Toolkit — Enterprise AI Agent Platform
added.note: NVIDIA's platform play for enterprise AI agents, adopted by Adobe, Salesforce, SAP, and 14 other major software vendors at GTC 2026.

added.title: Voicebox — Open-Source Voice Synthesis Studio
added.note: Trending open-source ElevenLabs alternative with 5 TTS engines, 23 languages, local-first privacy. 17.3k stars.

added.title: Kronos — Foundation Model for Financial K-Line Sequences
added.note: First foundation model for financial candlestick data, trained on 45+ exchanges. AAAI 2026 accepted.

added.title: Microsoft Copilot Health — Personal AI Health Companion
added.note: Microsoft enters consumer health AI, integrating wearables and 50k+ hospital records into personalized health insights.

added.title: Epic AI Agents — Art, Penny, and Emmie for Healthcare
added.note: Epic's three AI agents automate clinical documentation, revenue cycle, and patient self-service. 85% of Epic customers now using Epic AI.

updated.title: Hermes Agent v0.9.0 — Self-Improving AI Agent with Persistent Memory
updated.note: Updated from v0.8.0 to v0.9.0 (April 13, 2026 release). Stars jumped from 61.7k to 84.2k.

sweeps.summary: Added Open Agents by Vercel Labs — a forkable open-source cloud coding agent platform with 1.8k GitHub stars, live demo at open-agents.dev.

added.title: Open Agents — Open-Source Cloud Coding Agent Platform by Vercel Labs
added.note: Vercel Labs' reference implementation for cloud coding agents — full stack forkable repo with sandbox, durable workflows, and GitHub integration, actively promoted at open-agents.dev with 1.8k stars.

sweeps.summary: Parallel agent era: Claude Code Desktop gets drag-and-drop multi-session layout matching Cursor 3.1's tiled approach. Both now Editor's Choice — this is the new standard for AI coding tools.

added.title: Claude Code Desktop Redesign — Parallel Agents with Drag-and-Drop Layout
added.note: Same-day as routines launch: Claude Code Desktop now supports multi-session parallel workflows with Git worktree isolation, matching Cursor 3.1's tiled layout pattern.

sweeps.summary: Added email subscribe form to the header — daily digest signups via Buttondown. Enter your email, confirm, get AI releases in your inbox.
Evening sweep caught one hot drop: Anthropic's Claude Code routines launch, letting agents fix bugs and review PRs on a schedule or webhook trigger without needing your machine online.

added.title: Claude Code Routines — Scheduled Automations for Code Maintenance
added.note: Research preview of set-and-forget automations: configure a routine once and it runs nightly bug triage, code review, or deployment checks on Anthropic's infrastructure.

sweeps.summary: Targeted sweep of popular AI coding tools — Cursor 3.1 adds tiled agent layouts and voice input, GitHub Copilot cloud agent can now fix merge conflicts in three clicks, and Gemini CLI ships Chapters for organized long sessions.

added.title: Cursor 3.1 — Tiled Layout and Upgraded Voice Input
added.note: Point release adds parallel agent sessions in tiled panes, batch STT voice input, and 87% fewer dropped frames on large edits.

added.title: GitHub Copilot Cloud Agent — Fix Merge Conflicts in Three Clicks
added.note: New 'Fix with Copilot' button on github.com lets cloud agent resolve merge conflicts, validate tests, and push automatically.

added.title: Gemini CLI v0.37.1 — Chapters and Dynamic Sandbox Expansion
added.note: Chapters organize long agent sessions by tool usage; dynamic sandbox expansion supports restricted corporate environments.

sweeps.summary: Three additions focused on agent tooling and coding model upgrades — Google ADK adds auth providers and Gemma 4, Moonshot rolls out Kimi K2.6 with deeper reasoning, and MiniMax ships a multimodal CLI for AI agents.

added.title: Google ADK v1.30 — Auth Providers, Gemma 4, and BigQuery Tools GA
added.note: Google's agent framework adds auth provider support for registries, Gemma 4 model integration, and graduates BigQuery tools to stable.

added.title: Kimi Code K2.6 — Moonshot's Upgraded Coding Agent Model
added.note: Moonshot rolls out K2.6-code-preview with deeper reasoning traces and cleaner agent planning — early testers called it 'Opus-flavored.'

added.title: MiniMax MMX-CLI — Multimodal CLI for AI Agents
added.note: CLI giving agents native access to seven modalities — text, image, video, speech, music, vision, and search — without MCP integration.

sweeps.summary: First scheduled cron run after the 8h workflow went live — four items led by Tencent's open embodied foundation model family and the #1 trending HuggingFace paper of the day on cross-user agent skill evolution.

added.title: HY-Embodied-0.5
added.note: Tencent's first open-weights embodied foundation model — a 2B MoT plus a 32B MoE, purpose-built for robot VLA stacks, beating same-size peers on 16 of 22 benchmarks.

added.title: SkillClaw: Let Skills Evolve Collectively
added.note: Top HuggingFace paper of the day — a concrete framework for turning every user's agent trajectories into shared skill-library updates, exactly the 'gets better the more people use it' pattern agent products want.

added.title: Notebooks in Gemini (NotebookLM sync)
added.note: Google binds the Gemini app and NotebookLM through a shared project object — the first real 'workspace' surface inside Gemini, rolling out to paid tiers on web.

added.title: OpenWorldLib — unified world-model codebase
added.note: Apache-2.0 framework from a PKU/Kuaishou coalition that unifies interactive video, 3D, VLA and multimodal reasoning under one six-module inference stack — the kind of infra that makes next year's papers directly comparable.

sweeps.summary: Manual 30-day backfill to catch releases older than the 7-day cron window — 14 items including the full GPT-5.4 family launch, Google's Gemma 4 open models, Cursor 3.0, and Alibaba's Qwen 3.5-Omni and 3.6-Plus flagships.

added.title: Gemma 4 — open models for reasoning and agents
added.note: First Apache-2.0 model family that competes with frontier proprietary models on reasoning and agentic benchmarks while still fitting on a phone (E2B) or a single GPU (31B).

added.title: Cursor 3.0 — agent-native IDE
added.note: Major redesign centered on parallel agents in isolated git worktrees plus Design Mode — the first mainstream IDE to make parallel agent execution a first-class concept.

added.title: Qwen 3.6-Plus — agentic coding flagship
added.note: 1M-token native context, hybrid linear-attention MoE, and agentic coding performance that rivals Claude Opus 4.6 at ~1.7x the inference speed.

added.title: Qwen 3.5-Omni — native omnimodal model
added.note: Single model for text, images, audio and video with 113-language speech recognition and real-time voice cloning — the most complete omnimodal package from a Chinese lab.

added.title: MiMo-V2-Pro — Xiaomi's trillion-parameter agentic model
added.note: Xiaomi ships a 1T+ total / 42B active MoE that stealth-launched on OpenRouter as 'Hunter Alpha' and proved itself anonymously before the brand reveal — at $1/M input tokens.

added.title: GPT-5.4 mini and nano
added.note: Economy-tier GPT-5.4 variants — mini is free-tier fast and approaches the full 5.4 on SWE-Bench Pro; nano is API-only for high-volume classification and extraction.

added.title: Hindsight — agent memory that learns
added.note: MIT agent memory system modelled on human cognition — separate stores for facts, experiences and opinions, already top of LongMemEval and deployed at Fortune 500s.

added.title: DeerFlow 2.0 — open-source SuperAgent harness
added.note: ByteDance's MIT-licensed SuperAgent framework — orchestrates sub-agents, memory and sandboxes for long-running tasks. 60k+ stars.

added.title: AutoResearch — autonomous ML experiment runner
added.note: Karpathy's 630-line Python script that lets an agent design, run and iterate on ML experiments — ~100 per night. 70k+ stars in under three weeks.

added.title: GPT-5.4 — OpenAI's frontier model with native computer use
added.note: Main GPT-5.4 launch — the anchor release for the whole 5.4 family, with native computer-use tool built into the base model.

added.title: LTX-2.3 — open-source 4K video+audio generation
added.note: Open-weights 4K video generation with synchronized audio from a single prompt — rare to see a video model at this tier ship with open weights.

added.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite — cost-efficient multimodal model
added.note: Google's new cost-efficient tier in the Gemini 3.1 family for high-volume multimodal workloads.

added.title: Grok 4.20 Beta 2 — multi-agent reasoning with rapid learning
added.note: xAI's Grok 4.20 beta adds multi-agent reasoning paths and a rapid in-context learning loop.

added.title: Qwen 3.5 Small — on-device multimodal models
added.note: Small Qwen 3.5 variants tuned for on-device multimodal use cases.

sweeps.summary: Second cron sweep, six items — led by Alibaba's stealth video model HappyHorse-1.0 topping the Arena under a pseudonym, plus Microsoft's open multilingual embedding family Harrier and four trending open-source agent and memory projects.

added.title: HappyHorse-1.0 — Alibaba's Video Generation Model
added.note: Alibaba's stealth video model climbed to #1 on the Artificial Analysis Video Arena anonymously before the brand reveal — generates synchronized video and audio from one prompt.

added.title: Hermes Agent v0.8.0
added.note: Open-source self-improving agent with persistent memory and a built-in learning loop — 61.7k stars, ships across Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal and CLI.

added.title: OpenHarness — Open-Source Agent Infrastructure
added.note: MIT-licensed agent harness from HKUDS with 43 built-in tools, memory, multi-agent coordination and a personal-agent reference app.

added.title: oh-my-codex (OMX) — Multi-Agent Orchestration for OpenAI Codex
added.note: oh-my-zsh-style wrapper for OpenAI Codex CLI — adds parallel agent teams, persistent state and 33 specialized workflow prompts. 21k stars.

added.title: Harrier — Microsoft's Multilingual Embedding Family
added.note: MIT-licensed decoder-only embedding models (270M / 0.6B / 27B) from the Bing team — #1 on multilingual MTEB-v2 with 100+ language support and 32k context.

added.title: MemPalace — Local AI Memory System (Method of Loci)
added.note: Local MIT memory system that stores conversations verbatim and organizes them using a method-of-loci palace metaphor — 96.6% R@5 on LongMemEval at 170-token startup cost.

sweeps.summary: Manual backfill to populate the new tutorial, showcase and resource categories — seven items covering Netflix's VOID showcase, Google's Project Genie world-model demo, two official cookbooks, a free DeepLearning.AI short course and two high-traction awesome-lists.

added.title: VOID — Netflix's Video Object and Interaction Deletion Model
added.note: Netflix open-sources a video editing model that cleanly removes objects and the interactions they cause — a rare look at production film-tech as OSS.

added.title: Project Genie — Interactive AI World Generator
added.note: First shipped product powered by Google DeepMind's Genie 3 world model — type a prompt and walk around a generated 3D world at 24fps.

added.title: Awesome Agents — Curated List of AI Agent Tools
added.note: Well-organized directory of 80+ open-source AI agent frameworks, the fastest way to scan the landscape when evaluating a framework.

added.title: Building Agentic RAG with LlamaIndex (Free Course)
added.note: Free 44-minute DeepLearning.AI short course taught by Jerry Liu — one of the fastest on-ramps to agentic RAG with runnable code labs.

added.title: Awesome LLM Apps — 100+ Runnable Projects
added.note: The most-starred (~105k) collection of runnable LLM app examples on GitHub — complete projects across starter, multi-agent, voice, MCP and RAG categories.

added.title: Claude Cookbooks — Anthropic's Official Recipes
added.note: Anthropic's official Jupyter notebook collection covering tool use, RAG, sub-agents, prompt caching and the Claude Agent SDK. 38k stars, MIT.

added.title: OpenAI Cookbook — Official Examples and Guides
added.note: The canonical OpenAI API cookbook — maintained by OpenAI, covers every major API feature with runnable notebooks.

sweeps.summary: Third cron sweep, eight items — headlined by the Linux kernel's first official policy on AI-generated code, plus Liquid AI's tiny edge VLM, Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 going GA, and Google's on-device Gemma 4 tooling.

added.title: Linux Kernel Publishes Official Policy on AI-Generated Code
added.note: The world's most important open-source project now has official rules for AI-written contributions — a landmark ecosystem decision that every other major OSS project will reference.

added.title: LFM2.5-VL-450M — Tiny Edge Vision-Language Model
added.note: Liquid AI's 450M-parameter vision-language model tuned for edge and mobile deployment — competitive with models several times its size.

added.title: LiteRT-LM — Cross-Platform Edge LLM Inference
added.note: Google's open cross-platform inference framework for running LLMs on edge devices — the runtime side of the Gemma 4 on-device story.

added.title: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
added.note: Microsoft's production multi-agent orchestration framework hits 1.0 GA — enterprise agent infra from a major vendor reaching stable.

added.title: Graphify — Queryable Knowledge Graph From Any Folder
added.note: Turns any folder into a queryable knowledge graph for agent context — exactly the kind of utility that slots into existing agent memory stacks.

added.title: Google AI Edge Gallery — Run Gemma 4 Offline
added.note: Google's reference mobile app demonstrating Gemma 4 running fully offline on a phone — the consumer proof of the Gemma 4 / LiteRT-LM / edge story.

added.title: Trinity-Large-Thinking — Arcee's 400B Open Reasoning Agent
added.note: Arcee ships a 400B open-weights reasoning model tuned for agentic workflows — rare at this parameter count with open weights.

added.title: TRL v1.0 — HuggingFace Post-Training Library
added.note: HuggingFace's TRL library — the de facto RLHF/DPO/GRPO stack for post-training open models — reaches a stable v1.0.

sweeps.summary: Fourth cron sweep of the day — five items split between two fresh HuggingFace trending papers (Anuttacon's LPM 1.0 character video model and UCLA's OpenVLThinkerV2) and three GitHub-trending projects from this week, led by the breakout CLAUDE.md ruleset for Claude Code.

added.title: LPM 1.0 — Video-based Character Performance Model
added.note: First release from Anuttacon (the AI lab founded by miHoYo's Cai Haoyu) — a 17B diffusion transformer that drives full-duplex conversational video from a single character image at real-time speed.

added.title: OpenVLThinkerV2 — Generalist Multimodal Reasoning
added.note: UCLA NLP open-source vision-language reasoning model trained with Gaussian GRPO, a non-linear RL objective; reports 71.6% MMMU and 79.5% MathVista with full training code.

added.title: andrej-karpathy-skills — CLAUDE.md for Claude Code
added.note: Breakout GitHub trending repo this week (+4.9k stars) — a single CLAUDE.md file encoding four rules against common LLM coding failures, usable as a plugin or a one-liner curl.

added.title: Multica — Open-source managed agents platform
added.note: Self-hosted, vendor-neutral answer to hosted managed-agents services — assign issues to Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw or OpenCode on a shared board. 9.3k stars, +5.3k this week.

added.title: NVIDIA PersonaPlex — Real-time speech-to-speech
added.note: NVIDIA's open release of a 7B full-duplex conversational speech model with persona control — beats Gemini Live on dialog naturalness at 0.07s switch latency, trending on GitHub this week.

sweeps.summary: Quiet overnight sweep — two new model releases that filled remaining gaps in the 7-day window: MiniMax dropping open weights for its 229B agentic M2.7, and LG AI Research shipping its first open-weight vision-language model.

added.title: MiniMax M2.7 — open-weights self-evolving agent model
added.note: Frontier-tier 229B/10B-active MoE goes from API-only to downloadable weights under modified-MIT, with SWE-Bench Pro and Terminal Bench scores in the same neighborhood as GPT-5.3-Codex.

added.title: EXAONE 4.5 — LG's first open-weight vision-language model
added.note: LG AI Research extends the EXAONE family to multimodal with a 33B VLM, 262K context, six-language support and day-one TensorRT-LLM / vLLM / SGLang / llama.cpp coverage.

sweeps.summary: Added MemPalace via manual proposal — updated the existing entry with corrected release date, bumped importance to major, refreshed star count to 44.5k, added a third link, and promoted it to editor's choice.

added.title: MemPalace — Local AI Memory System (Method of Loci)
added.note: Viral MIT memory system co-created by Milla Jovovich — 44.5k GitHub stars in under a week, 96.6% LongMemEval score, and the local-first alternative to Mem0/Zep that the community has been asking for.

sweeps.summary: Quiet afternoon sweep — two high-quality gap-fills from the last week that prior sweeps missed: Ai2's promptable 3D detector WildDet3D and Hugging Face's Sentence Transformers v5.4 adding native multimodal embeddings and rerankers.

added.title: WildDet3D — Promptable monocular 3D detection in the wild
added.note: Ai2's open-world 3D detector — one RGB image in, 3D boxes out, with text/box/point prompts and a new 1M-image dataset across 13K+ categories. Tops Omni3D, Argoverse 2 and ScanNet zero-shot, ships with an iPhone app.

added.title: Sentence Transformers v5.4 — multimodal embeddings and rerankers
added.note: The standard Python library for embeddings adds native multimodal support — text, images, audio and video go through the same SentenceTransformer/CrossEncoder API, with Qwen3-VL, BGE-VL, Llama Nemotron and Jina rerankers working out of the box.

sweeps.summary: Tuesday sweep — three fresh picks spanning a GitHub-shipped MCP registry install flow in Copilot CLI v1.0.25, the viral claude-mem plugin that gives Claude Code persistent memory, and a top-trending Shanghai AI Lab paper pushing back on the 'SFT memorizes, RL generalizes' narrative.

added.title: GitHub Copilot CLI v1.0.25 — MCP Registry Integration
added.note: GitHub's official terminal agent gains direct MCP registry installs plus remote sessions and ACP-client MCP pass-through — exposes 'apt-get for agents' UX to anyone with a GitHub login.

added.title: claude-mem — Persistent Memory Plugin for Claude Code
added.note: 54k-star community answer to Claude Code's missing memory — local ChromaDB + SQLite, file-read gating, and v12.1's Knowledge Agents for queryable observation corpora.

added.title: Rethinking Generalization in Reasoning SFT
added.note: #1 trending Hugging Face paper (306 upvotes) with 33 open models and 4 released datasets; argues long-CoT SFT does generalize once you fix optimization, data quality, and base-model strength.

sweeps.summary: Retry sweep after this morning's scheduled run failed mid-job. The 7-day window was already heavily covered, so this pass only adds one item — OpenAI Codex CLI v0.120 with Realtime V2 background streaming — which the prior sweeps missed.

added.title: OpenAI Codex CLI v0.120 — Realtime V2 background streaming
added.note: Official OpenAI terminal agent (75k stars, Rust) ships a 30-PR point release: the background-agent pipeline streams incremental progress while work is running and queues follow-up turns — earlier sweeps covered v0.119's WebRTC voice switch but missed v0.120.

sweeps.summary: Empty retry sweep after the 11:44 scheduled run failed on its first attempt. Two hours after the prior sweep, no new releases in the 7-day window cleared the bar — top HF trending papers (Matrix-Game 3.0, OmniShow, RefineAnything) either trace back to March repo commits or lack an in-window primary source, and GitHub trending is still led by repos already in the feed.

Retry sweep after the 15:09 run's first attempt failed — four items that earlier sweeps missed: Google DeepMind's Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 with instrument-reading capabilities, NVIDIA's first open quantum AI models, AWS Agent Registry for enterprise agent governance, and Intel OpenVINO 2026.1 with a preview llama.cpp backend.

added.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 — instrument reading and multi-view reasoning
added.note: Google DeepMind's embodied reasoning model adds 93% instrument-reading success and a Boston Dynamics collaboration for facility inspection — a practical robotics capability milestone from a top lab.

added.title: NVIDIA Ising — open AI models for quantum error correction and calibration
added.note: NVIDIA's first open AI models target quantum hardware infrastructure: a 35B MoE VLM for automated QPU calibration and a CNN delivering 2.5x faster error correction, already adopted at Harvard and Fermilab.

added.title: AWS Agent Registry — governed catalog for AI agents and MCP servers
added.note: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore's new catalog solves AI agent sprawl for enterprises — semantic search, approval workflows, IAM governance, and an MCP-server interface so agents can query the catalog at runtime.

added.title: OpenVINO 2026.1 — llama.cpp backend for Intel CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs
added.note: The preview llama.cpp backend in this quarterly release gives Intel hardware users a single optimized inference path across CPU, Arc GPU, and NPU without patching separate projects.

sweeps.summary: Added Shep via manual proposal workflow — an open-source parallel AI coding agent orchestrator that runs multiple agents in isolated git worktrees.

added.title: Shep — parallel AI coding agent orchestrator across git worktrees
added.note: MIT-licensed CLI and dashboard for running Claude Code, Cursor, Codex and other agents in parallel across git worktrees, with full prompt-to-PR automation and CI auto-fix.

sweeps.summary: Retry sweep after the 16:59 run's first attempt failed — two items not covered by earlier sweeps today: Anthropic's advisor tool beta (Haiku+Opus doubles BrowseComp to 41.2%) and NVIDIA's Audio Flamingo Next (beats Gemini 2.5 Pro on MMAU-Pro). Also fixed a sort regression introduced by the manual proposal that had placed shep-ai-shep at position 0 despite its Feb date.

added.title: Anthropic Advisor Tool — Pair Sonnet or Haiku with an Opus Advisor
added.note: New Claude API beta that lets cheap executor models call Opus 4.6 mid-task for strategic planning — Haiku with Opus advisor more than doubled its BrowseComp score (19.7% → 41.2%) at 85% lower cost than Sonnet.

added.title: Audio Flamingo Next — Open Audio-Language Model with 30-Minute Temporal Reasoning
added.note: NVIDIA and UMD's April 13 open-weight audio model beats Gemini 2.5 Pro on both MMAU-Pro and LongAudioBench — filling the feed's gap in audio AI.

sweeps.summary: Retry sweep picking up 16 items from April 7–10, 2026 missed by the previous failed run — highlights include GLM-5.1 (open 754B MoE beating GPT-5.4 on SWE-Bench), Claude Mythos Preview (Anthropic's unreleased frontier model), Muse Spark (Meta's first post-reshuffle model), Waypoint-1.5 (realtime world simulation at 60fps), and platform launches including Claude Managed Agents and Claude Cowork GA.

added.title: Hippo Memory — biologically-inspired memory for AI agents
added.note: MIT-licensed agent memory system modeled on hippocampal mechanisms with episodic/semantic two-speed storage, configurable decay, and sleep-based consolidation.

added.title: GLM-5.1
added.note: Open-weight 754B MoE model from Z.ai with 58.4 SWE-Bench Pro score — ahead of GPT-5.4 and Opus 4.6 under an MIT license.

added.title: Claude Mythos Preview (Project Glasswing)
added.note: Anthropic's most capable unreleased model, loaned to a 12-partner coalition for critical-software vulnerability research — already found a 27-year-old bug.

added.title: RAGEN-2: Reasoning Collapse in Agentic RL
added.note: Diagnoses 'template collapse' in multi-turn RL agents and proposes an SNR-aware prompt filter that lifts reasoning across planning, math, web nav, and code.

added.title: Claw-Eval: Toward Trustworthy Evaluation of Autonomous Agents
added.note: 300-task benchmark grading agents on full trajectories — catches 44% of safety violations that final-output-only grading misses.

added.title: TriAttention — trigonometric KV cache compression
added.note: Attention scheme exploiting Q/K vector concentration in pre-RoPE space to compress the KV cache and cut long-context inference memory.

added.title: Claude Managed Agents (Public Beta)
added.note: Anthropic's managed agent harness with sandboxed containers, persistent sessions, and multi-agent coordination at $0.08/session-hour on top of token costs.

added.title: Muse Spark
added.note: First model from Meta Superintelligence Labs post-reshuffle — natively multimodal, matches Llama 4 Maverick at an order of magnitude less compute.

added.title: OpenClaw — self-hosted personal AI assistant
added.note: MIT-licensed personal AI assistant bridging any LLM to 20+ messaging channels, now past 355k GitHub stars.

added.title: Safetensors joins the PyTorch Foundation
added.note: The de-facto ML serialization replacement for pickle moves to vendor-neutral governance under the Linux Foundation.

added.title: caveman — Claude Code skill that compresses chat output
added.note: Claude Code skill stripping filler from agent chat output while leaving code untouched — reports 22–87% token savings, averaging ~65%.

added.title: ALTK-Evolve — continuous learning for AI agents
added.note: IBM Research's long-term memory system converting agent trajectories into reusable guidelines so agents learn from their own runs.

added.title: OpenSpatial — 3M-sample spatial-intelligence data engine
added.note: Open-source data engine plus 3M-sample dataset for 3D spatial reasoning, yielding ~19% average relative improvement on spatial benchmarks.

added.title: Claude Cowork — General Availability with Enterprise Controls
added.note: Claude Cowork exits research preview with RBAC, group spend limits, usage analytics, OpenTelemetry tracing, and a Zoom MCP connector on macOS and Windows.

added.title: Waypoint-1.5
added.note: Real-time interactive world model running at 720p/60fps on RTX 5090 — a live video simulation navigable with keyboard and mouse.

added.title: Archon — deterministic workflow engine for AI coding agents
added.note: Open-source YAML-defined workflow engine with deterministic phases, isolated git worktrees, and composable nodes mixing scripts with AI tasks — 16.6k stars.

sweeps.summary: Quiet overnight window — checked GitHub trending, Hacker News, HuggingFace, and Product Hunt. No new releases within the last 6 hours cleared the notability bar that aren't already in the feed.
Quiet window — nothing new in the last 6 hours cleared the bar. Recent sweeps have captured the notable releases well; GitHub trending and HN front page showed only items already in the feed.
Two additions from the April 14-15 window: Google Chrome Skills (Gemini prompt automation in-browser) and ClawGUI (open-source mobile GUI agent framework from ZJU, 333 stars in 48h).

added.title: Google Chrome Skills — Reusable Gemini Prompt Workflows in One Click
added.note: Real product launch from Google shipping Gemini prompt automation into Chrome — 152+ HN upvotes and a pre-built Skills Library confirm genuine interest.

added.title: ClawGUI — Unified Framework for Training, Evaluating, and Deploying GUI Agents
added.note: First open-source full-stack GUI agent pipeline (RL training + reproducible benchmarking + real-device deployment) from ZJU-REAL; 333 GitHub stars and 307 HuggingFace Papers upvotes in under 48 hours.

sweeps.summary: Quiet morning sweep — one notable open-weight drop: Baidu's ERNIE-Image, an 8B Diffusion Transformer for text-to-image with a focus on text rendering and complex layouts, released publicly on HuggingFace today.

added.title: ERNIE-Image — Baidu's Open-Weight Text-to-Image Diffusion Transformer
added.note: Baidu's first standalone open-weight text-to-image model, released publicly on HuggingFace April 15; trending in top 10 on HuggingFace with strong text-rendering benchmarks and Apache 2.0 license.

sweeps.summary: Quiet midday window — checked GitHub trending, HuggingFace papers, HN front page, and major lab blogs. No new releases passed the quality bar in this 6-hour window; all candidate items were either already in the feed or released before the since threshold.

Two additions this sweep: NVIDIA Lyra 2.0 (released today, explorable generative 3D worlds with Isaac Sim export) and DDTree (diffusion draft trees for speculative decoding, 8.22× speedup over autoregressive inference, beats EAGLE-3).

added.title: Lyra 2.0 — NVIDIA's Explorable Generative 3D World Framework
added.note: NVIDIA Spatial Intelligence Lab released Lyra 2.0 today with 889 GitHub stars; generates persistent 3D worlds from video diffusion and exports Gaussian Splats directly to Isaac Sim.

added.title: DDTree — Diffusion Draft Trees for Faster Speculative Decoding
added.note: 177 HuggingFace upvotes and 8.22× speedup over autoregressive decoding; beats EAGLE-3 on math benchmarks with a novel tree-based draft from block diffusion.

sweeps.summary: Quiet overnight window — checked GitHub trending, Hacker News, HuggingFace trending models, and HuggingFace papers. All candidates from the past 6 hours were already in the feed.
Quiet early-morning window — checked GitHub trending, Hacker News, HuggingFace trending models and papers, and lab blogs. No new releases in the past 6 hours passed the notability bar.

One item from yesterday's window that earlier sweeps missed: Eigen Labs' Darkbloom, a decentralized private inference network running on idle Apple Silicon Macs — 204 HN points, hardware-backed privacy via Secure Enclave, and ~50% cheaper than centralized providers.

added.title: Darkbloom — Private AI Inference on Idle Macs by Eigen Labs
added.note: Launched April 15 with 204 HN points but missed by all prior sweeps — decentralized Mac inference network with hardware-backed privacy is a novel concept from a well-known infrastructure org.

sweeps.summary: One April 15 item missed by all prior sweeps: Gemini CLI v0.38 ships subagents, letting the orchestrator delegate focused tasks to specialist agents with isolated context windows, tool sets, and @agent syntax.

added.title: Gemini CLI Subagents — Delegate to Specialist Agents Directly from Your Terminal
added.note: Announced April 15 in the Google Developers Blog and missed by all prior sweeps today; the subagents feature adds a multi-agent delegation pattern to Gemini CLI with 4 built-in specialists and @agent syntax for explicit routing.

sweeps.summary: One solid drop from today's Cloudflare Agents Week: Email for Agents (public beta) gives Workers-based AI agents native email send and receive — 265 HN points and the most novel launch of the window.

added.title: Cloudflare Email for Agents — Native Bidirectional Email for AI Agents (Public Beta)
added.note: Released April 16 as part of Cloudflare Agents Week — native email for AI agents is a genuinely novel capability with 265 HN points, enabling agents to participate in enterprise email workflows without requiring users to change how they communicate.

sweeps.summary: One strong pick from today's window: Alibaba's Qwen3.6-35B-A3B — a 35B total / 3B active MoE open-weight model under Apache 2.0 that tops the HN front page with 292 points and posts 73.4 on SWE-bench Verified.

added.title: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B — Open-Weight Agentic Coder with 3B Active Params
added.note: Released April 16 with 292 HN points; 73.4 SWE-bench Verified and 51.5 Terminal-Bench 2.0 are among the highest open-weight scores at this active-parameter class, under Apache 2.0.

sweeps.summary: Two items from Cloudflare's Agents Week: Artifacts (Git-compatible versioned storage for agents, private beta) and AI Platform (AI Gateway expanding to 70+ models across 12 providers with automatic failover).

added.title: Cloudflare Artifacts — Git-Compatible Versioned Storage for AI Agents
added.note: Released April 16 as part of Cloudflare Agents Week — fills a real gap for coding agents that need versioned, branchable file storage with Git semantics at scale.

added.title: Cloudflare AI Platform — Unified Inference Layer Across 70+ Models and 12 Providers
added.note: Released April 16 as part of Cloudflare Agents Week — 174 HN points; AI Gateway's expansion to a full multi-provider inference layer addresses a real pain point for teams managing multiple model providers.

sweeps.summary: Two items added: OpenAI's Codex desktop app v26.415 brings computer use, Atlas browser, and image generation to paid ChatGPT users; ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 paper tops HuggingFace trending as the day's #1 paper with its joint audio-video generation architecture.

added.title: OpenAI Codex v26.415 — Computer Use, In-App Browser, and Image Generation
added.note: Released April 16 with 449 HN points — adds genuine computer use and Atlas in-app browser to the Codex desktop app, moving it from code-only to full desktop agent with 3M weekly active users and 70% month-over-month growth.

added.title: Seedance 2.0 — ByteDance's Unified Audio-Video Generation Model
added.note: Published April 15 and trending as #1 paper on HuggingFace today (109 upvotes); missed by all prior sweeps — the joint audio-video generation architecture is a meaningful step for the video generation field.

sweeps.summary: Two solid Cloudflare Agents Week drops from April 17 added — Unweight (lossless LLM compression via Huffman-coded BF16 exponents, 22% bundle reduction) and Agent Memory (managed persistent cross-session memory for Workers-based AI agents). GitHub trending, HN, and HuggingFace were also checked; all other candidates were either already in the feed or below the notability bar.

added.title: Cloudflare Unweight — 22% Lossless LLM Compression via Huffman-Coded BF16 Exponents
added.note: Novel production-deployed lossless compression technique with concrete metrics (22% bundle reduction, ~3 GB VRAM savings on Llama 3.1 8B) — technically interesting to ML infrastructure engineers.

added.title: Cloudflare Agent Memory — Managed Persistent Memory Service for AI Agents (Private Beta)
added.note: Practical managed memory layer for AI agents with five-channel retrieval and RRF fusion — directly useful to developers building long-running agents on Workers.
